publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2019-12-09 00:07:33 +0000.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

origination.label: creator
origination.persname: White, E. B. (Elwyn Brooks), 1899-1985. (Title of work: Family that dwelt apart..)

did.physdesc: Manuscripts, letters, documents, clippings, photographs, filmstrips, film reels, notecards, cassette tapes, bound photocopies, medals, and awards.
did.abstract: Manuscripts, letters, documents, clippings, photographs, filmstrips, film reels, notecards, cassette tapes, bound photocopies, medals and awards by, to, or about E.B. White, spanning the entire range of his activities throughout his life.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Manuscripts, letters, documents, clippings, photographs, filmstrips, film reels, notecards, cassette tapes, bound photocopies, medals and awards by, to, or about E.B. White, spanning the entire range of his activities throughout his life. Letters consist of ca. 3,000 letters by White and 25,000 letters to him from others; manuscripts include drafts, notes, and some galley proofs for his books Charlotte's Web, The Elements of Style, the Essays of E.B. White, the Letters of E.B. White, One Man's Meat, The Points of My Compass, Poems and Sketches, and Stuart Little, as well as drafts of his "Notes and comment" and "Talk of the Town" columns for the New Yorker magazine from 1934-1953. Printed items include White's contributions to the New Yorker and other magazines, his editorials for his high school newspaper, and ca. 350 articles about him, including interviews and reviews. Photographs include a large portrait photo of White, ca. 1950, and a family group photo taken at the 50th wedding anniversary of Samuel T. White and Jesse White in 1929, with E.B. and Katherine Sergeant White included in the group. Films include Maine Lobsterman, narrated by White and produced for the television show Omnibus in 1954, and The Family That Dwelt Apart, written and also narrated by White. Also included in the collection are objects such as E.B. White's typewriter, desk, and table.

Correspondents include: Katharine Sergeant White (his wife), James Thurber, Frank Sullivan, Harold Ross, William Shawn, Gus Lobrano, S.J. Perelman, Nathaniel Benchley, Howard Cushman, Alice Burchfield Sumner, Scott Elledge, and publishers such as Harper & Row, Farrar, Straus, and Giroux, and Macmillan.

relatedmaterial.head: RELATED MATERIALS
relatedmaterial.p: Book collection also held at the repository, including virtually all editions of White's books. Related manuscript and book collections also held at the repository concerning New Yorker writers A.J. Liebling, Frank Sullivan, and James Thurber.

arrangement.head: COLLECTION ARRANGEMENT
arrangement.p: Organized into the following major series: Manuscripts by White; miscellaneous printed material; letters received by White; letters written by him; fan letters received by him; and other materials.

Manuscripts arranged alphabetically by book title; letters received by White, alphabetically by correspondent; letters written by White, in chronological order; fan letters received, alphabetically by subject.

arrangement.head: SERIES LIST
arrangement.p: Series II. Correspondence Boxes 59-219; 260-261; 266 Outgoing Correspondence Incoming Correspondence Miscellaneous and Solicitations Readers Mail from Harper's Incoming Correspondence from Readers
Series III. Photographs, Printed Material, and Whiteiana Boxes 220A-230; 260; 266 Photographs Printed Material Whiteiana
Series IV. Medals, Awards and Clippings Boxes 231-240; 258; 260; Mapcase Folder 3 and 4 Medals and Awards Clippings
Series V. Harper and Row Files Boxes 241-243
Series VI. Macmillan Publishing Company files related to Elements of Style Box 267
Series VII. Books on E.B. White by Others Boxes 244-257; 259; Mapcase Folder 2 Scott Elledge, E.B. White: A Biography Katherine Romans Hall, E.B. White: A Bibliographic Catalogue
Series VII. Miscellaneous (mainly oversize) Boxes 260 and 262; Museum Items

Series VIII. Adaptations, Films, and Recordings Boxes 260; 264-266; Mapcase Folder 1 Adaptations and Recordings of Charlotte's Web Adaptations and Recordings of Stuart Little Films and Recordings (not based on books)

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: E. B. White collection, #4619. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

accessrestrict.head: Access Restrictions:
accessrestrict.p: Access restricted to the permission of the curator.

userestrict.head: Restrictions on Use:
userestrict.p: Researchers may view one folder at a time.

did.unittitle: Essays of E. B. White galley proofs
One Man's Meat-Onward and Upward in
Points of My Compass - final draft and postscript

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes printed thank you card with poem sent to Sam Fink after death of White's wife (dated August 6)

did.unittitle: New Yorker Book Ross, Harold Wallace

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Note on envelopes read "Some included in the book, some not"

did.unittitle: Cornell Dept. of Rare Books A-H

Photographs of Cass Caufield and Bill Maxwell from the National Book Committee
Photograph of Honorary Award recipients at Harvard (includes E.B. White)
Photograph of E.B. White and the White Family
The Newspaper Guild of New York Page One Literature Award for "The Second Tree from the Corner"

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Loose photocopies of clippings and articles

did.unittitle: NY Times clipping listing Pulitzer Prize winners, including E.B. White
Macmillan Publishing Company files related to Elements of Style

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains files originally held by MacMillan Publishing company and includes correspondence, manuscripts, drafts, reviews, and clippings related to the publications of the first three editions of The Elements of Style.

did.unittitle: Elements of Style2nd edition - revised preface
Elements of Style 1st edition - notes and feedback
Elements of Style 1st edition - discarded material
Elements of Style 1st edition - uncorrected proof

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes correspondence related to later editions.

scopecontent.p: Copy of the original booklet printed in Ithaca, NY.

scopecontent.p: Letter to his alma mater Williams College and from Katherine White - grammar related.
